section.heading: Annual report on comprehensive nuclear-test-ban treaty sensors

subsection.heading: Requirement
subsection.chapeau: Not later than 90 days after December 27, 2021, and not later than September 1 of each subsequent year, the Secretary of State shall submit to the appropriate congressional committees a report on the sensors used in the international monitoring system of the Comprehensive Nuclear-Test-Ban Treaty Organization. Each such report shall include, with respect to the period covered by the report—

paragraph.content: the number of incidents where such sensors are disabled, turned off, or experience “technical difficulties”; and

paragraph.chapeau: with respect to each such incident—

subparagraph.content: the duration of the incident; and
whether the Secretary determines there is reason to believe that the incident was a deliberate act on the part of the host nation.

subsection.heading: Appropriate congressional committees defined
subsection.chapeau: In this section, the term “appropriate congressional committees” means—

paragraph.content: the Committee on Foreign Affairs and the Committee on Armed Services of the House of Representatives; and
the Committee on Foreign Relations and the Committee on Armed Services of the Senate.

note.heading: Codification
note.p: Section was enacted as part of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2022, and not as part of the Arms Control and Disarmament Act which comprises this chapter.
